title: Amorphophallus teuszii
taxonomy: Amorphophallus teuszii is a species of flowering plant in the family Arum. It is a member of the genus Amorphophallus, which includes over 200 species of aroid plants. This species is native to the tropical regions of Cameroon and is characterized by its large, inflorescent flowers. The classification of Amorphophallus teuszii places it within the order Alismatales, which includes various aquatic and terrestrial plant species. Further classification also groups it within the class Liliopsida, also known as monocotyledonous flowering plants.

intro: Amorphophallus teuszii, also known as Teusz's Titan Arum, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant belonging to the Arum family (Araceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Amorphophallus teuszii include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
